reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–O bond rearrangement in nitrile oxide generation; loss of H2O from ethyl nitroacetate

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents premixed in DES; phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization to furoxan. No incompatibility between starting materials at room temperature, so a single feed is appropriate. DES is viscous at RT so the feed reservoir should be gently warmed (~40-50°C) to ensure pumpability.

reagents.name: Ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive reaction to completion

reagents.name: Tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.role: Deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond acceptor)
reagents.notes: Forms DES with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium promoting nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: Ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.role: Deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond donor)
reagents.notes: Forms DES with TBAB; 1 mL total DES used per 0.5 mmol substrate

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ. The DES medium facilitates this transformation through hydrogen-bond-mediated activation.

Regioselective [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide 1,3-dipole with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile). The oxygen of the nitrile oxide attacks the terminal carbon of the alkyne, and the carbon of the nitrile oxide bonds to the internal carbon, forming the isoxazole ring with 5-phenyl regiochemistry.
Aromatization/tautomerization of the initial cycloadduct to give the fully aromatic isoxazole product (ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate).

stream_logic.reasoning: Single premixed feed: both substrates dissolved in DES. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms to trap it before dimerization. No catalyst or base needed. DES feed reservoir warmed to ~40-50°C for pumpability (viscosity reduction). Concentration: phenylacetylene at 0.5 M, ethyl nitroacetate at 1.0 M in 1 mL DES.

mixing_order_reasoning: Both substrates must be premixed before entering the heated zone. The nitrile oxide intermediate is highly reactive and will dimerize to furoxan if phenylacetylene is not immediately available as a trapping agent. A single premixed stream ensures the dipolarophile is present at the moment of nitrile oxide generation. No sequential addition is needed.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species or radicals involved; thermal pericyclic mechanism is unaffected by O2.
quench_reasoning: Reaction reaches completion in 15 min at 120°C. In flow, simply cooling the output stream to room temperature effectively stops the reaction. Batch protocol quenches with water for workup purposes only, not for chemical quenching.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate and [3+2] cycloaddition. The DES viscosity is a practical concern for flow — TBAB/EG (1:5) has significant viscosity at RT (~50-100 cP), requiring heated feed lines or dilution. Pressure drop through narrow-bore tubing with viscous DES should be calculated using Hagen-Poiseuille; a wider-bore coil (e.g., 1.0-1.6 mm ID) may be needed. The 15 min residence time at 120°C is well-suited for flow. Uncertain whether DES recycling is feasible in continuous mode. The 2.0 equiv excess of ethyl nitroacetate compensates for competitive nitrile oxide dimerization; in flow with better mixing and heat transfer, it may be possible to reduce to 1.5 equiv.
